# wterm vendored bundle

Experimental third renderer alongside xterm.js and restty. Selectable via
**Settings → Terminal Renderer → wterm** in Den.

## Upstream

- Source: https://github.com/vercel-labs/wterm
- Packages: `@wterm/dom` and `@wterm/core` (both pinned to the same version in the root `package.json`)
- License: Apache-2.0

## Files

| File | What it is | Editable? |
|------|-----------|-----------|
| `wterm.bundle.js` | esbuild ESM output of `@wterm/dom` + `@wterm/core` (WASM inlined as base64). | **Do not edit** — regenerated by `npm run build:wterm`. |
| `wterm.css` | Concatenation of upstream `@wterm/dom/src/terminal.css` + `wterm.den.css`. | **Do not edit** — regenerated by `npm run build:wterm`. |
| `wterm.den.css` | Den-specific CSS overrides (themes, scroll tweaks, etc.). | Edit this for any project-specific styling; the build script preserves it. |
| `wterm-xterm-adapter.js` | Facade that wraps `WTerm` with an xterm.js-compatible API so `terminal.js` / `float-terminal.js` work unchanged. | Hand-maintained. |

## Rebuilding

```bash
npm install          # if lockfile changed
npm run build:wterm  # regenerates wterm.bundle.js + wterm.css
```

Then bump the single source of truth `WTERM_VERSION` in `wterm-xterm-adapter.js`
and the matching `?v=N` query in `frontend/js/terminal-adapter.js` so browsers
pick up the new bundle (`/vendor/wterm/wterm-xterm-adapter.js?v=N`).

## Updating the upstream version

1. Update `@wterm/core` and `@wterm/dom` in the root `package.json` **to the same pinned version** (e.g. `"0.1.9"` — no caret, to prevent silent drift since upstream is 0.1.x experimental).
2. `npm install`
3. `npm run build:wterm`
4. Bump `WTERM_VERSION` in the adapter and `?v=N` in `terminal-adapter.js`.
5. Manually verify: input/output, resize, theme switch, iOS soft keyboard, scrollback, Shift+PageUp/PageDown/Home/End, tap-to-copy select mode.

## Known upstream-coupling notes

- The adapter calls `WTerm` with `autoResize: false` and clears the element's
  `style.height` after `init()` to override `_lockHeight()`. This is a private
  WTerm detail — if upstream reorders initialization, reverify.
- The adapter moves the hidden input textarea to `document.body` after init and
  styles it `position: fixed; bottom: 0; font-size: 16px;` to avoid iOS Safari
  focus-scroll behavior and auto-zoom. If upstream changes the textarea's
  lifecycle (e.g. recreates it on resize), this needs to be re-applied.
